feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to create a sequence number in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

How to create a sequence number in SAP Datasphere transformation flow just like how we create in Informatica?

Thanks,
Samrat Muddasani

entry.title: Hierarchy with directory issue when sharing with other spaces
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

In ADMIN Space : 

I created a hierarchical with directory and associated it with the Master Dimension. Then I associated the master dimension with the fact. so I created Analytics model from the fact and dispplay data.

I able to set hierarchy for Cost Center. then I share the fact, the masters and the hierarchy to HR space.

In HR Space :

I Created Analytics Model from the Fact and previewd the data.

I found below error 

I try to unshare the Hierarchy with Directory so that this space does not have a Hierarchy with Directory. Then I tried previewing data; it worked, but I couldn't set the hierarchy.

Do you have any solutions for this problem?

entry.title: Exposed Data access in Power BI and Other Clients, Tools, and Apps via ODBC
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm in a team developing SAP Datasphere in my workplace, and we're having some concerns about the Exposed Data that can be consumed via ODBC, we compleated the process to configure this conection and obtained access to all the data products in the Spaces required, but according to SAP Help, via ODBC we can only have access to Views (Data builder)and Perspectives (Bussiness Builder), while in practice we can see and use also the Analytic Model (Data Builder). This concern us because we couldn't find any documentation about this extention on the capabilities or the possible restrictions that might come with it.

 

We would appreciate your help in this, to be sure about this topic and the capabilities that may be added in the connection via ODBC. This way, we would be sure that this is the solution we've been looking for or we might need to look for other kind of connection to access all the Exposed Data available in SAP Datasphere.

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: How to resize column width in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello All,

I see an option to resize the columns individually in SAP Analytics Cloud.

When do we expect the same feature in SAP Datasphere? Particularly, it will help developers to see more objects with less scrolling.

Below is the screenshot from SAP Analytics Cloud.

Thanks!

Regards,

Mandar Damle

entry.title: I dream with a "Parameterized" Task Chain
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, I dream with a "parameterizable task chain", where I can feed a variable with for example a company code, and that this value is passed as parameter to the data flows and these pass it to the views and finally to the data sources.

Now that I wake up from my dream 😂, I have to see how I can emulate this (so basic in an ETL) in Datasphere.

Since Task Chains have no parameters, and parameterized views cannot be used in Data Flows, it gets complicated.

I have thought of using a local table as a way to pass parameters between views, that means that a TC has a DF that insert into this table the Company Code to be loaded, and the nexts DFs and Views in the Task Chain read that table to know what they need to load.
BUT how to manage multiple executions (for example when 1 company is loading, then another execution tries to load another company, and things like that?

In SAP Data Services (on-prem ETL from SAP) we can use the JOB_RUN_ID, that is an unique identifier of the task, so if we save in a table the job_run_id and the value, all the steps on this job can read the table correctly filtering by job_run_id. So the process 1 is loading one company, and the process 2 another company .. all works fine.

BUT in DSP there is no JOB_RUN_ID, ate least there is no SQL Function to get it, and to be able to use in a DF or view., so I can't identify different procesess or executions. I tried using the SESSION context, or another HANA DB function, but without success.

Any idea, inspiration, experience, etc... doing something like this in Datasphere? I'm missing something?

Thanks!

entry.title: User's management in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello all

I have a situation in project I'm working on regarding Azure-AD manage users and Datasphere control.
We have SAML configured and it's working fine, all new users included in Azure AD groups are generated automatically in DataSphere as expected.

However, I'm facing with problem when the user is excluded from AD groups in Azure. I did not see how the same user is excluded from DataSphere.
Does any one has the same problem and of course any thoughts for a solution?

entry.title: SAP Analytics for Cloud and SAP Ariba - Data Connections
entry.content:
Dear All,

Looking at the SAP Standard Content on SAC for Ariba, when it comes to data connection for SAC and Ariba, I could see there are two different ways data can be brought into SAC from Ariba.

1. Using Datasphere

2. Using oData Connections.

However, when I look at some of the SAP Learnings, the oData Connection part requires a kind of Data Lake created in SAP BTP and then SAP BTP providing an oData Connection to transfer this data to SAC.

My query is, why do we need two different types of connections. Can't the stories (like Contract Management ) also have the data coming from Datasphere? 

If someone could explain the technical limitations / designs behind this approach that would be a great help.

Thank you,

Srinivas

entry.title: Configurate the connection between S4 OP and Datasphere
entry.content:
Could you provide a step through guide for configurate the connection between S4 OP and Datasphere?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Business Builder in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

Does Business Layer make any sense now as the recomendation of SAP is to use analytics models for consumption? I am a little bit confused and it is not clear enough in which situations we should use Business Layers. Initially they were recommended for self-service but I think the business users have to create too many layers, which makes it too complex. The analytic model is not so complex, so I guess the users can be empowered to create their own analytic models. What is SAP position regarding the self-service? 

I'm looking forward to hearing your thoughts.

Thank you!

Cristina

entry.title: Integration Datasphere - S/4HANA On-premise
entry.content:
Hi,

What is your experience with the integration between Datasphere and S/4HANA and how did you manage the delta uploads? Have you created your data model in Datasphere based on CDS views or based on SAP extractors? It seems there are lot of CDS views which are not enabled for delta extraction which makes the things a little bit difficult. What is the SAP position regarding this integration? Shall we use cds view or extractors as data sources?

entry.title: Connection issue - SAP DataSphere
entry.content:
Hello All,

The connections (The trail system for DataSphere ) gives error when I try to expand it in the – Graphical CV Editor

The same connections are working fine , in the DataFlow creation Editor .

Could you please explain , why ?

 

 

 

 

Thanks

Kiran

entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Need your valuable opinion on below scenario,

In Datasphere is there any way i can achieve the above result?

Regards,

Amith

entry.title: Error deleting rows in local table with data editor
entry.content:
I am having a problem trying to delete rows from a local table with the data editor.
I always get INTERNAL SERVER ERROR.

After some research, I think the problem is that the table has a field with a technical name that is "GROUP", and I guess that the part of the program that does the DELETE, does not escape the names of the fields with double quotes and being GROUP a reserved word in SQL the DELETE FROM T WHERE GROUP = 1 statement fails.
update: well, a DELETE with a field named "VALUE" works great, so seems that the problem is just with the name GROUP. 

If you encounter this error, maybe it will help you 🙂

Regards,

entry.title: How to assign system Owner role to a service account?
entry.content:

What do I need to do?

entry.title: Filter with Hierarchy Structure
entry.content:

In SAP BW 

we can select hierarcy

We can expand hierarchy and select value.

entry.title: Connection between SAP SAC and Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a trial account in Datasphere, and I am unable to establish a successful connection between Datasphere (DS) and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Even if the connection is established, I cannot access the respective Space present in DS. None of my objects in DS are visible in SAC when trying to create a connection between them.
Kindly help out with this issue.
Going to SAC connections and choosing Datasphere under the live connections to create a connection:

And then I entered the host and HTTPS port details. The port being  443 and the host being my DS trial account

Choosing the connections here

GE81390I is the connection that I have established between SAC and DS, yet this error shows up whenever I try to pull data from the DS for story creation

Thank you!

 



Please assist in the problem
Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Connection between SAP SAC and Datasphere
SAP Datasphere Catalogue
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am just getting started with the Datasphere catalogue and have 2 questions I hope you can help me with.

1. I am trying to define an approach to cataloguing with Datasphere but cannot find any real world examples on how best to apply it.

For example we would like to catalogue our SAC models for the end users and so we have ongoing governance and a central location for technical specs and updates for the developers side. Where should developers add their technical build details about SAC models? e.g. queries, loads, joins etc. Should this go in the SAC catalogue description or in the Description field under Datashpere catalogue? or do folks leave off the technical details and log these elsewhere if so what are you using to do this?

For us we would prefer keep all details in one place as more sustainable.

2. If I add descriptions to a model in SAC  the info flows through into the Datasphere catalogue but not  if I do it the other way round? why?

 Also its the same for any tags, if I add them in either place they don't carry across?

Regards,

L

entry.title: Explore Datasphere via Excel - options
entry.content:
Hi,

I have some inquiries regarding the SAC add-in for Microsoft and SAP Analysis for Office, particularly concerning the use of Datasphere. I’ll tell you what my understanding is, but I need your confirmation:

  1. SAC Add-in for Microsoft – it is included with the SAC Enterprise subscription, can’t be  utilized without a SAC subscription(?). It enables access to both Datasphere tenant (views, analytic models, perspectives etc) and SAP Analytics Cloud tenant (Datasphere objects via Datasphere live connection and SAC import models).
  2. Analysis for Office:
    • SAC Edition – it is included with the SAC Enterprise subscription, can’t be  utilized without a SAC subscription(?). It enables access to both Datasphere tenant (views,  perspectives, NO ACCESS TO ANALYTIC MODELS etc) starting from the version 2.8 SP14 and to SAP Analytics Cloud tenant (Datasphere objects via Datasphere live connection except analytic models and SAC import models).
    • Standard Edition – included with BO Enterprise and BPC subscriptions or it can be acquired as standalone version. It enables access to both Datasphere tenant (views, perspectives, NO ACCESS TO ANALYTIC MODELS etc) and SAP Analytics Cloud tenant (Datasphere objects via Datasphere live connection except analytic models and SAC import models).

Please, let me know if my understanding is correct.

Thank you

Cristina

entry.title: Data replication in SAP Datasphere's cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm in a team developing SAP Datasphere in my workplace, we've been investigating about the use of the data products on the Data Builder and Bussiness Builder, we want to know if every data product created is replicated in SAP Datasphere when is deployed, or is it only the logic behind it. We can't replicate databases on cloud sources because of our security policy.

We know that there are products that are meant to be replicated by using the the Data Integration Monitor, but in our case we would only use the ones that might not be replicated like views, analytic models, intelligent lookup and entity relationship models.

We're using SAP HANA OP as our data warehouse and only using remote tables so none is replicated in SAP Datasphere, in case you mention that every data product is replicated in SAP Dataspheres's cloud, is there an use case that helps us mitigate this problem and continue the project?

Thank you in advance,
